ops.body:
Phase 1 of the XRPC plan (we-want-to-expose-ancient-raven) shipped. The daemon op surface is now generated from one table.

Where: `orchestrate/registry.go` (table + adapter + error codes), `orchestrate/schema.go` (reflection to JSON-schema).

Contract for later phases:
- A `method{name,kind,desc,exposes,reqType,resType,adapt}` is built by generic `query[Req,Res](...)` / `procedure[Req,Res](...)`. The socket op string IS the method name (`cco.<noun>.<verb>`, camelCase verbs). Add a method → append to the `methods` slice; `registerOps`, MCP tools, and (phase 2) XRPC pick it up automatically.
- `expose` bits: `full`=socket|xrpc|mcp, `rpc`=socket|xrpc, `sock`=socket only. `cco.agent.report` is sock-only; the new show/list/unset getters are `rpc` (off parent MCP).
- Error wire format: handlers return `(Res, error)`; `opErr(code, err)` tags it and the socket adapter serializes `"<Code>: <message>"` in `Reply.Error`. Codes: InvalidRequest|NotFound|Conflict|Unsupported|InternalError. Untagged → InternalError; store `errNotFound` sentinel → NotFound (via `replyError`). Phase 2 XRPC bridge parses this prefix back to an HTTP status.
- Strict decode: `decodeBody[Req]` uses DisallowUnknownFields; empty body → zero Req. `runTyped` is the shared adapter (tests call it too).
- MCP tool name derivation: `mcpName` = strip `cco.`, dot to underscore, camel to snake. Schemas are generated from req structs (per-field human descriptions dropped; method `desc` is the tool description).

Not yet added (later phases own them): cco.fleet.status, cco.agent.capture, cco.agent.respawn, cco.sprint.kill. Table is trivially appendable.

Breaking: all old op strings ("agent-spawn" etc.) are gone; agent-status → cco.agent.show (MCP agent_status → agent_show). Single-user, wire renamable.
